design_philosophy.core_loop: Pre-mission squad composition and loadout selection → tactical deployment → real-time unit coordination using terrain, flanking, suppressive fire, and stealth to complete mission objectives
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource constraint (fixed squad slots and limited special abilities per mission) vs. diverse tactical challenges requiring different unit compositions
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Field commander orchestrating specialized military units in real-time tactical combat, where careful planning and tactical positioning matter more than economic dominance
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Explicitly rejects RTS resource/economic management mechanics in favor of pure tactical gameplay. No reinforcements mid-mission forces upfront squad selection to be consequential. Faction asymmetry in unit design (Crayven heavy armor/ballistics vs. Order hover/energy) creates distinct playstyles

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through squad composition (unit type selection), equipment specialization (limited special weapons/equipment per squad), and tactical decision-making (flanking, high ground positioning, suppressive fire, stealth awareness)

progression.upgrade_system: Progressive unlocking of dropship capacity (maximum of 3 depending on campaign progress, each carrying up to 4 squads). Squad customization through weapon and equipment selection increases options
progression.unlock_mechanism: Campaign progression unlocks additional dropship slots and potentially new unit types/equipment options for load-out screens
replayability_hooks: Faction asymmetry encourages replaying campaign with different faction playstyles
Squad composition puzzle-solving for each mission
Multiplayer mode with dynamic squad selection

world_building_techniques: Briefing screens between maps establish mission context
Faction conflict framing (two factions competing for planetary control and alien artifacts)
Distinct faction identities through unit design philosophy and aesthetics

faction_indicators: Crayven Corporation (heavy armor, traditional ballistics, movement-focused)
Order of the New Dawn (hover propulsion, energy weaponry, speed/mobility-focused)
danger_signals: Unit armoring weaknesses (tanks vulnerable to side/rear/top attacks)
Suppressive fire mechanics incentivize combined arms awareness
Friendly fire damage from rear-line support creates coordination challenges
environmental_storytelling: High ground tactical advantage mirrors real military doctrine
Terrain shadows enable stealth camouflage positioning
Foliage concealment for infantry units suggests natural cover mechanics
